sources.source: Hassler et al. 2014, Science 343:1244797 (MSL RAD); solar-cycle range per Guo et al. 2021, Astron. Astrophys. Rev. 29:8
sources.note: Primary named doc is Hassler et al. 2014, Science 343:1244797 -> DOI 10.1126/science.1244797 (title 'Mars' Surface Radiation Environment Measured with the Mars Science Laboratory's Curiosity Rover'), confirmed valid via Crossref. Publisher landing (science.org) is paywalled/403 to bots but is the canonical DOI target. The secondary Guo et al. 2021 review is linked separately under its own source string (DOI 10.1007/s00159-021-00136-5).

sources.source: NASA-STD-3001 Vol 1 Rev B (2022), per 2021 NAS review; universal all ages/sexes, 3% mean REID basis
sources.note: Official standards.nasa.gov landing page for NASA-STD-3001 Volume 1 (Crew Health); confirmed live. Page now lists Rev C as current active revision; Rev B (2022) is the cited revision and its signed PDF is available under the standard's /B/ path (set as mirror_pdf_url).

sources.source: NASA BVAD NASA/TP-2015-218570 Rev2 (2022), Table 3-28; ISS standard workweek
sources.note: Life Support Baseline Values and Assumptions Document (BVAD), NTRS 20210024855, published 2022-02-01 (BVAD_2.15.22-final.pdf). NTRS citation permalink confirmed live; direct PDF set as mirror.

sources.source: NASA BVAD NASA/TP-2015-218570 Rev2 (2022), Table 3-28 (Langston 2005, ISS)
sources.note: Same BVAD document (NTRS 20210024855). Replaced prior source_url (the /api/.../downloads/ PDF) with the stable citation permalink; PDF kept as mirror.

sources.source: NASA BVAD NASA/TP-2015-218570 Rev2 (2022), Table 3-28
sources.note: Same BVAD document (NTRS 20210024855). Citation permalink used as stable landing page.

sources.source: NASA BVAD NASA/TP-2015-218570 Rev2 (2022), Table 3-28 (1.0 h per meal x 3)
sources.note: Same BVAD document (NTRS 20210024855).

sources.source: NASA BVAD NASA/TP-2015-218570 Rev2 (2022), Table 3-28 (DPC 0.5 + plan review/report 1.0 + work prep 0.5)
sources.note: Same BVAD document (NTRS 20210024855).

sources.source: NASA BVAD NASA/TP-2015-218570 Rev2 (2022), Table 3-28 (2.0 h on each of 2 weekend days)
sources.note: Same BVAD document (NTRS 20210024855).

sources.source: NASA BVAD NASA/TP-2015-218570 Rev2 (2022), Table 3-29 and sect. 3.2.5
sources.note: Same BVAD document (NTRS 20210024855).

sources.source: NASA BVAD NASA/TP-2015-218570 Rev2 (2022), sect. 4.5.3 (Shuttle experience 45-90 min/d for crew of 6)
sources.note: Same BVAD document (NTRS 20210024855).

sources.source: NASA BVAD NASA/TP-2015-218570 Rev2 (2022), sect. 4.5.3 (Hunter model; LMLSTP Phase III measured 4.6 CM-h/d for crew of 4)
sources.note: Same BVAD document (NTRS 20210024855).

sources.source: Russell & Klaus 2007, Reliab. Eng. Syst. Saf. 92:808-820 (ISS/Mir-derived)
sources.note: Russell & Klaus 2007, 'Maintenance, reliability and policies for orbital space station life support systems', Reliability Engineering & System Safety 92(6):808-820. DOI 10.1016/j.ress.2006.04.020 confirmed valid via Crossref. Replaced prior ScienceDirect abstract-PII url with the canonical DOI (Elsevier full text is paywalled).

sources.source: Stromgren et al. 2021, IEEE Aerospace, NTRS 20210026843 (lunar Surface Habitat, ISS component data: expected 24.7 h per 28-day 2-crew mission; 50th pct 9.0 h, 99th pct 70.5 h)
sources.note: Stromgren et al., 'Assessment of Crew Time for Maintenance and Repair Activities for Lunar Surface Missions' (IEEE Aerospace), NTRS 20210026843. Citation permalink confirmed live; used as stable landing, prior /api download PDF kept as mirror.

sources.source: NASA HAT EVA team constraint, in Stromgren et al. 2015, NTRS 20150009469
sources.note: NTRS 20150009469, 'Developing a Crew Time Model for Human Exploration Missions to Mars' (Battfeld, Stromgren, Shyface, Cirillo, Goodliff, 2015). Citation permalink confirmed live; prior /api download PDF kept as mirror.

sources.source: NASA EVA-EXP-0031 figures cited in Griffin, AIAA 2020-4170 (58.25 h prep + 15.83 h post, typically 2-person EVA)
sources.note: Named doc is Griffin, AIAA 2020-4170, 'The Wait-less EVA Solution: Single-Person Spacecraft' (Brand N. Griffin, ASCEND 2020). AIAA DOI 10.2514/6.2020-4170 confirmed valid via Crossref. NOTE: prior source_url http://spacearchitect.org/pubs/AIAA-2020-4170.pdf is DEAD (DNS SERVFAIL / ENOTFOUND) - replaced with the stable AIAA DOI landing page (arc.aiaa.org is paywalled/403 to bots but is the canonical target).

sources.source: Estimate: exploration atmosphere 56.5 kPa/34% O2 gives ~15-min prebreathe (Abercromby et al. 2015, Acta Astronautica 109:76-87) + don/doff, checkout, dust mitigation, amortized suit servicing
sources.note: Named doc inside an 'Estimate:' string -> Abercromby, Conkin & Gernhardt 2015, 'Modeling a 15-min extravehicular activity prebreathe protocol using NASA's exploration atmosphere (56.5 kPa/34% O2)', Acta Astronautica 109:76-87. Correct DOI is 10.1016/j.actaastro.2014.11.039 (confirmed via Crossref; NOT .12.009). Prior source_url was the ScienceDirect abstract-PII page (S0094576514004937, same paper, paywalled); replaced with canonical DOI.

sources.source: NASA BVAD NASA/TP-2015-218570 Rev2 (2022), Table 4-69 (lunar surface EVA values)
sources.note: Same BVAD document (NTRS 20210024855).

sources.source: NASA BVAD NASA/TP-2015-218570 Rev2 (2022), Table 4-69 (polar 0.25-0.5; equatorial 0.46-0.76)
sources.note: Same BVAD document (NTRS 20210024855).

sources.source: Whitmire et al. 2015, NASA/TM-2015-218564 (SME consensus, NTRS 20140016951); consistent with NASA-STD-3001 Vol 2 task-based NHV requirement
sources.note: NTRS 20140016951, Whitmire et al., 'Minimum Acceptable Net Habitable Volume for Long-Duration Exploration Missions SME Consensus Session Report' (report JSC-CN-32284). Citation permalink confirmed live; PDF available at /api/.../20140016951.pdf (mirror).

sources.source: Whitmire et al. 2015 consensus report (5.4 m3 allocation); ISS USOS CQ flight actual 2.1 m3 (Broyan et al., 08ICES-0222)
sources.note: Primary named doc is the same Whitmire et al. 2015 NHV consensus report (NTRS 20140016951, confirmed live). Secondary Broyan et al. 08ICES-0222 is a conference paper not separately linked; the NHV consensus report is the citable landing page.

sources.source: Zeitlin et al. 2013, Science 340:1080-1084 (MSL RAD cruise, 2011-12)
sources.note: Zeitlin et al. 2013, 'Measurements of Energetic Particle Radiation in Transit to Mars on the Mars Science Laboratory', Science 340:1080-1084. DOI 10.1126/science.1235989 confirmed valid via Crossref. Prior source_url (science.org) is paywalled/403 to bots but is canonical DOI target.

sources.source: NASA-STD-3001 Vol 1 Rev B [V1 4031], per OCHMO-TB-020 Rev E (12/2022); legacy 30-day BFO 250 mGy-Eq limit is NCRP-132/pre-Rev-B and Townsend et al. 2018
sources.note: Named doc OCHMO-TB-020 Rev E: NASA-STD-3001 Technical Brief 'Design for Ionizing Radiation Protection', OCHMO-TB-020 Rev E. Verified by reading the PDF (title page confirms OCHMO-TB-020 Rev E, references [V1 4031] Short-Term Radiation Limits - Solar Particle Events). NASA-hosted direct PDF with no separate landing page; set as both url and mirror_pdf_url.

sources.source: Guo et al. 2021, Astron. Astrophys. Rev. 29:8; Ralha et al. 2024, arXiv:2409.02001
sources.note: Primary named doc Guo et al. 2021, 'Radiation environment for future human exploration on the surface of Mars: the current understanding based on MSL/RAD dose measurements', Astronomy & Astrophysics Review 29:8. DOI 10.1007/s00159-021-00136-5 confirmed valid via Crossref (open access on SpringerLink). The prior source_url pointed to the secondary Ralha et al. 2024 (arXiv:2409.02001, 'Effective dose equivalent estimation for humans on Mars', confirmed live); replaced with the primary Guo review DOI.

sources.source: Röstel et al. 2020, JGR Planets 125:e2019JE006246 (GEANT4/AtRIS, RAD-validated): equivalent dose peaks at 30-40 cm (88-102 g/cm2) and only drops below surface value beyond ~200 g/cm2 (0.7-0.9 m dry rock)
sources.note: Röstel, Guo, Banjac, Wimmer-Schweingruber & Heber 2020, 'Subsurface Radiation Environment of Mars and Its Implication for Shielding Protection of Future Habitats', JGR Planets 125:e2019JE006246. DOI 10.1029/2019JE006246 confirmed valid via Crossref. Prior source_url (agupubs wiley) is paywalled (402) to bots but is canonical DOI target.

sources.source: Röstel et al. 2020, Table 3: 1.26 m andesite (rho=2.8 g/cm3, ~350 g/cm2) reduces water-sphere equivalent dose to 200 mSv/yr, <=half the dry-surface value; sandstone 1.64 m
sources.note: Same Röstel et al. 2020 JGR Planets paper, DOI 10.1029/2019JE006246 (confirmed via Crossref). Canonical DOI used in place of paywalled agupubs page.

sources.source: Röstel et al. 2020 (0.8-2.5 m depending on composition; hydrated less); Zhang et al. 2022 JGR Planets: 1-1.6 m at representative sites
sources.note: Primary named doc is Röstel et al. 2020 JGR Planets, DOI 10.1029/2019JE006246 (confirmed via Crossref). Secondary Zhang et al. 2022 JGR Planets is not separately linked here; Röstel is the primary citable landing page.

sources.source: Townsend et al. 2018, Life Sci. Space Res. 17:32-39 (PMID 29753411); Kim et al. 2006: 5 g/cm2 Al insufficient for BFO in Aug-1972-class event; ~10 g/cm2 meets 30-day BFO limits; 20+ g/cm2 (hydrogenous) for extreme events
sources.note: Primary named doc Townsend et al. 2018, 'Solar particle event storm shelter requirements for missions beyond low Earth orbit', Life Sciences in Space Research 17:32-39 (PMID 29753411). PubMed landing page confirmed live (matches title/authors/pages). DOI is 10.1016/j.lssr.2018.02.002; PubMed permalink kept as the stable landing already present and verified.

sources.source: Crucian et al. 2016, Int. J. Gen. Med. 9:383-391 (ISS incidence; skin rash 1.12/py, upper-respiratory 0.97/py leading; infections ~0.6/py)
sources.note: Crucian et al. 2016, 'Incidence of clinical symptoms during long-duration orbital spaceflight', Int. J. Gen. Med. 9:383-391. PMC5098747 open-access landing page confirmed live (matches title/authors/pages).

sources.source: Derived estimate from Antonsen et al. 2022 Fig. 4 (EVAC 0.3-1% per <42-d mission, 4 crew, unlimited ISS capability); not a stated IMM rate
sources.note: Although phrased 'Derived estimate', the string NAMES a real doc: Antonsen et al. 2022, 'Estimating medical risk in human spaceflight', npj Microgravity 8:8. DOI 10.1038/s41526-022-00193-9 confirmed valid via Crossref. Type=document. Prior source_url (nature.com article page) redirects through auth; canonical DOI used (open access on nature).
